steps.title: Identify what exponential growth means
steps.content:
Exponential growth occurs when a quantity multiplies by a constant factor each period.

If a bacteria population grows exponentially, the count at each hour is the previous count times some factor k (where k > 1).

This produces the pattern: Start with $B_0$, after 1 hour: $B_0 \cdot k$, after 2 hours: $B_0 \cdot k^2$, and so on.

The key is multiplication by a constant ratio, not addition of a constant amount.

steps.title: Classify each choice as multiplicative or additive
steps.content:
Review each answer choice:
  • Choice A: 16% greater means multiply by 1.16 each hour. This is multiplicative.
  • Choice B: 1,600 greater means add 1,600 each hour. This is additive.
  • Choice C: 1,600 less means subtract 1,600 each hour. This is additive.
  • Choice D: 1,600 less means subtract 1,600 each hour. This is additive.
Only a multiplicative process (constant multiplier) produces exponential growth. Additive processes (constant difference) produce linear growth.

steps.title: Select the exponential model
steps.content: Since exponential growth requires a constant multiplicative factor, only choice A fits the description. Choice A states the population increases by 16% each hour, which means multiplying by 1.16 each hour—the hallmark of exponential growth. That matches choice A — A is correct.
